name: Irish Creme Fudge (Microwave)
recipeInstructions:
Fudge gets its texture from the potato: Cook potatoes in a microwave oven for about 4-5 minutes at full power until tender. Mash the cooled mashed potato and stir it with the liqueur until smooth. Set aside.
In a separate bowl, melt chocolate and margarine in a 2-quart microwave-safe casserole dish on high for 1-2 minutes. Stir to combine.
Slowly stir the potato mixture into the chocolate mixture. Add powdered sugar gradually, stirring or kneading until smooth. Press the fudge into an 8x8x2-inch foil-lined pan and cut it into thirty-six 1-1/4 inch squares. Place a walnut piece in each square.
For icing: In a small microwave-safe bowl, melt margarine on high for about 45 seconds. Beat in sifted powdered sugar until smooth. Stir in enough Irish Creme liqueur (1-2 tablespoons) to achieve an icing consistency that can be piped or drizzled over the fudge.
This recipe yields 36 pieces. Each serving contains approximately 91 calories, 0.5 grams protein, 15.7 grams carbohydrate, 3.1 grams fat, and 0 milligrams of cholesterol with 15.7 milligrams sodium. Calories from fat account for 30 percent of the total.
